banco_oraciones.oracion: I have an apple and a banana.
She is a teacher at the school.
The sun is very strong today.
There is an iguana in the garden.
He isn't a doctor; he is an engineer.
Is there a supermarket near here?
Is she a teacher? Yes, she is.
Do you have a pet? No, I don't.
Is this the last arepa? Yes, it is... and it's mine.

interaccion: Sofía: I am **a** designer. Ben is **a** photographer... **an** unlucky photographer.
Ben: Hey! I saw **an** iguana this morning. It was in **the** garden, next to **the** mango tree.
Kenji: **The** sun is strong today. I need **a** hat and **an** umbrella.
Abuela Carmen: In this house there is **a** rule: **the** last arepa is for **the** grandmother.
inicio.definicion_pragmatica: Articles present a noun: a/an for one thing among many (any teacher), the for the specific one everybody knows (the sun) — and English needs them even where Spanish doesn't.

filas: a banana, a hat, a university
an apple, an iguana, an hour
the sun, the last arepa, the garden
English needs it: She is A teacher

ejercicios.afirmacion: We choose 'an' by the sound, not the letter: an apple, an hour.
In English, jobs don't need an article: 'She is doctor' is correct.

ejercicios.texto: There is ___ iguana in the garden. (a / an / the)
Kenji needs ___ hat for the sun. (a / an / the)
___ last arepa is for the grandmother. (a / an / the)

ejercicios.respuesta: She is a teacher at the school
Is there a supermarket near here

ejercicios.texto_con_error: An sun is very strong today.
ejercicios.respuesta: The sun is very strong today.

ejercicios.oracion_base: There is an iguana in the garden.
ejercicios.respuesta: Is there an iguana in the garden?

ejercicios.pregunta: Do you have a pet? (short answer)
Is the sun a planet? (short answer, No)

guion: Kenji: Sofía, is there a supermarket near here?
Sofía: Yes, there is one on the corner, next to the park.
Kenji: Great. I need an umbrella and a hat. The sun is terrible today.
Ben: Buy an ice cream too! There is a machine at the entrance.
Sofía: Ben, you are a genius... an ice cream genius.

reading.texto: Kenji writes in his diary: 'Today I saw an iguana for the first time. It was in the garden of the house, under a mango tree. Abuela Carmen says the iguana visits every morning and eats a flower — always one flower, always at the same time. It is a very organized animal. In Japan, I had a cat. A cat is a good friend, but an iguana with a schedule? That is a Colombian surprise.'

preguntas.afirmacion: Kenji saw an iguana in the garden.
The iguana eats three flowers every day.
Kenji had a cat in Japan.
The iguana visits at a different time every day.
